_basis: IEC 60364-4-44 covering Sections 441 (supply disturbances), 442 (HV/LV faults), 443 (atmospheric and switching overvoltages), 444 (EMC)
_note: AMD2:2018 significantly expanded the mandatory scope of SPD installation. SPDs are now required for all new installations unless a risk assessment (per IEC 62305-2) demonstrates they are not needed. In practice, SPDs should be specified on all new installations. This file covers Section 443 (SPDs for lightning and switching) as it applies most frequently in design.

overvoltage_categories_IEC_60664_1._purpose: Equipment is assigned a category based on its position in the installation — the category determines the rated impulse withstand voltage (Uw). SPDs must limit surges to below Uw of the protected equipment.

category_IV.description: Equipment at the origin of the installation — energy meters, overhead line termination equipment, main switches. Must withstand the highest surges before any SPD protection.

category_III.description: Fixed equipment in the fixed installation — distribution boards, circuit breakers, wiring systems. Must survive after Type 1 SPDs have acted.

category_II.description: Equipment connected at socket outlets or fixed equipment that uses socket/plug connection. This is the typical equipment withstand level.
examples: Class I or II equipment plugged in

category_I.description: Protected equipment — sensitive electronics, telecommunications, signal processing equipment. Requires the highest level of surge suppression.

type_1.purpose: At service entrance — handles the full energy of a direct lightning strike or close indirect strike to the incoming line.
type_1.installation_location: Main distribution board (MDB) or service entrance, where the supply enters the building
mandatory_when: Building has an external LPS (Lightning Protection System) — risk of partial lightning current injection
Building supplied by overhead lines (not underground cable)
Risk assessment per IEC 62305-2 indicates it is required
type_1.protection_level_Up: Typically 2.5–4 kV (must be < Uw of category III equipment = 4kV)
type_1.coordination_note: Type 1+2 combination devices are common — provide both direct lightning and induced surge protection at the main board

type_2.test_current_waveform: 8/20 μs — simulates induced/conducted surges from nearby lightning
type_2.test_parameter: In (nominal discharge current) in kA, and Imax (maximum discharge current)
type_2.purpose: At main and sub-distribution boards — handles conducted and induced surges not absorbed by Type 1 (or provides protection in the absence of Type 1).
type_2.installation_location: Every distribution board — main DB and all sub-distribution boards (SDBs)
mandatory_when: AMD2:2018: All new installations unless risk assessment justifies omission
Any TT or TN-S installation supplied by lines with any overhead section
Where sensitive electronics (Category I equipment) are used
type_2.protection_level_Up: Typically 1.0–2.0 kV (should be < Uw of category II equipment = 2.5 kV)

type_3.test_current_waveform: 1.2/50 μs voltage, 8/20 μs current combined wave
type_3.purpose: Point-of-use supplementary protection for Category I (sensitive) equipment.
type_3.installation_location: In or adjacent to the protected equipment — UPS output, server rack PDU, sensitive instrument panel
type_3.note: Type 3 SPDs cannot be installed alone without Type 2 upstream — they have insufficient energy handling capacity. Must be a coordinated system.
type_3.protection_level_Up: Typically 0.5–1.0 kV (must be < Uw of category I equipment = 1.5 kV)
typical_forms: Plug-in surge protection (BS 1363 socket with SPD)
IEC socket outlet with SPD built in

type_1_plus_2_combination.description: Single device performing the function of both Type 1 and Type 2. Convenient for main boards where both levels of protection are needed.
type_1_plus_2_combination.advantage: Single device, no coordination distance requirement between Type 1 and Type 2 stages.
type_1_plus_2_combination.typical_application: Main distribution board at service entrance, particularly for buildings with LPS or overhead supply.

Uc_continuous_operating_voltage.definition: The maximum AC RMS (or DC) voltage the SPD can be connected to continuously without damage or spurious operation.
Uc_continuous_operating_voltage.minimum_requirement: Uc ≥ 1.1 × U0 (allows for supply voltage tolerance)
Uc_continuous_operating_voltage.for_400V_line_to_line: Uc ≥ 440V — specify ≥ 460V for line-to-line connected SPDs
Uc_continuous_operating_voltage.note: Connecting an SPD with insufficient Uc to the supply will cause permanent conduction and fire risk

Up_protection_level.definition: The peak voltage passed through (clamped to) by the SPD during a surge test at rated current.
Up_protection_level.requirement: Up must be less than the rated impulse withstand voltage (Uw) of the protected equipment
Up_protection_level.margin: Apply a 20% margin: Up ≤ 0.8 × Uw for good practice
Up_protection_level.for_category_II_equipment: Up ≤ 2.5 × 0.8 = 2.0 kV — Type 2 SPDs typically achieve 1.0–2.0 kV
Up_protection_level.for_category_I_equipment: Up ≤ 1.5 × 0.8 = 1.2 kV — requires Type 3 at point of use

In_discharge_current.definition: The current the Type 2 SPD is designed to discharge without degradation.
In_discharge_current.minimum_recommended: 5 kA (8/20μs) for residential, 10 kA for commercial
In_discharge_current.for_high_risk_areas: 20 kA for buildings in high lightning-density areas (keraunic level > 20 days/year)
In_discharge_current.for_Africa_note: Many African countries have high keraunic levels (> 30–60 days/year). Specify 10 kA minimum for all installations, 20 kA near transformers.

Iimp_for_type_1.definition: Impulse current the Type 1 SPD handles in the 10/350 μs test (direct lightning simulation).
Iimp_for_type_1.minimum: 12.5 kA per pole where LPS is present, 10 kA where LPS is absent
Iimp_for_type_1.typical_product: 25–50 kA per pole for robust protection

mandatory_installation_scope_AMD2_2018._note: AMD2:2018 changed the default from 'SPDs not required unless risk assessment shows otherwise' to 'SPDs required unless risk assessment shows they are not needed'.
always_required: Buildings supplied by any overhead line sections (partial or full overhead from transformer to consumer)
Buildings in rural areas where supply cables are overhead
Buildings with an external LPS (Type 1 or Type 1+2 at main board)
Installations containing Category I equipment (servers, medical instruments, sensitive controls)
required_by_default_unless_risk_assessme: Extensions and rewires of existing residential
Any installation in a country where IEC 60364-4-44 AMD2 has been adopted

omission_criteria.when_risk_is_low: Only where risk assessment per IEC 62305-2 demonstrates that the risk of loss without SPDs is below the tolerable risk level. This typically applies to: buildings fully supplied by underground cables with no overhead connection, in areas of very low lightning ground flash density (< 1 flash/km²/year), and where the consequence of loss is low (no critical equipment, no fire risk).
omission_criteria.practical_note: In most real projects, the cost of SPDs is low relative to the cost of damage or loss. Do not omit SPDs to save money unless the risk calculation explicitly supports it.

connection_leads.requirement: SPD connection conductors (from busbar to SPD) must be as short as possible.
connection_leads.reason: Long leads add inductance — the inductive voltage drop during a fast surge adds to the protection level Up, degrading effectiveness.
connection_leads.maximum_total_lead_length: Ideally ≤ 0.5m total (live + earth). Absolute maximum 1.0m total for Type 2. This is the total length of the connection path — not one conductor.
connection_leads.practical_installation: Mount SPD on DIN rail in the distribution board. Use short jumper leads. Route as directly as possible to earth bar.

overcurrent_protection.requirement: SPDs must be protected against the fault current that could result from a failed SPD (short-circuit mode failure).
overcurrent_protection.gG_fuse_or_MCB_sizing: Per SPD manufacturer's instructions — typically 63A gG fuse or 63A Type B MCB per SPD module for the earth fault current path.
overcurrent_protection.note: Some SPD products include built-in thermal disconnect — confirm with manufacturer whether additional protection is needed.

earth_connection.requirement: SPD earth terminal must connect to the installation earth (PE) at the board. For TN-C-S installations, connect to PEN at the MET or to PE bar at the DB.
earth_connection.for_TT_installations: Connect to the local earth bar — the SPD provides a low-impedance path from line to earth during surge, which also causes RCD operation. Use time-delayed (S-type) RCDs or select an SPD with separate PE connection to avoid RCD nuisance tripping.

between_type_1_and_type_2.minimum_cable_distance: If using separate Type 1 and Type 2 devices, minimum 10m of cable between them (or a decoupling inductor of ≥ 1.5 μH). Without this, the two devices do not coordinate — the Type 2 may fail from energy intended for Type 1.
between_type_1_and_type_2.solution: Use a Type 1+2 combination device — no coordination distance needed.

between_type_2_and_type_3.note: Type 3 at socket outlets typically satisfies this naturally.

TT_systems_and_RCDs.issue: SPD operation discharges current to earth, which appears as a residual current to the RCD, potentially causing nuisance tripping.
TT_systems_and_RCDs.solution_1: Use Type S (time-delayed) 300mA RCDs at the main board — the delay allows the surge to pass before the RCD responds. Final circuit 30mA RCDs typically do not cause a problem as SPDs are installed upstream.
TT_systems_and_RCDs.solution_2: Use SPDs with separate earth connection path that bypasses the RCD.
TT_systems_and_RCDs.solution_3: Install SPD upstream of the main RCD (at the service head — consult DNO).

photovoltaic_systems.requirement: SPDs are required on both DC (PV string/array) and AC (inverter output) sides. DC SPDs must be rated for DC voltage (≥ 1.2 × Voc of string). SPD at inverter DC input and SPD at AC output at main board.

EV_charging.recommendation: SPD on the supply to EV charging point. EV onboard chargers contain sensitive electronics (Category I). Specify Type 2 at the charging circuit sub-board.

worked_example._title: SPD specification for a 3-phase commercial office building in Nigeria
worked_example.context: 5-storey office building, TN-C-S supply (underground cables from transformer), Lagos area, keraunic level ~50 days/year, no external LPS, building has servers and BMS

risk_assessment_summary.sensitive_equipment: Yes — servers (Category I), BMS controllers (Category I)
risk_assessment_summary.consequence_of_loss: High — business continuity risk, data loss
risk_assessment_summary.conclusion: SPDs required — AMD2:2018 default, confirmed by sensitive equipment and high keraunic level

at_main_distribution_board.type: Type 2 (or Type 1+2 combination for belt-and-braces given 50-day keraunic level)

at_server_room_PDU.note: Verify ≥ 5m cable from floor SDB Type 2 SPD to server room. Install decoupling inductor if shorter.
